title: lavandula canariensis subsp. palmensis
intro: Lavandula canariensis subsp. palmensis, also known as the Palmensis Lavender or Grandes Lavandes, is a subspecies of the Canarian lavender plant, native to the Canary Islands and specifically found on the island of La Palma. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are due to its high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate. The health benefits of Lavandula canariensis subsp. palmensis include its ability to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, and improve sleep quality, making it a popular choice for aromatherapy and herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which can be used topically or inhaled to reap its numerous benefits.
